ux_issues.description: The shared trip view (/shared/{token}) returns raw JSON rather than a rendered HTML page. For non-technical users like Priya sharing with friends, this is not useful.
ux_issues.suggestion: Return a rendered HTML page or redirect to the frontend app for the shared view.

ux_issues.description: The AI created a 6-day itinerary (days 1-6) when asked for 5 days (June 15-20). Day 6 covers June 20 checkout/departure, which is technically correct since checkout is at noon, but the user said '5 days'. Minor mismatch in how days are counted.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider whether departure day should be a separate day or folded into the last full day.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: When asked 'When is my cooking class?', the AI correctly noted no cooking class was booked (only Sagrada Familia tour was ingested). However, the persona description mentions a pre-booked paella cooking class. This is a test design issue, not an AI issue — the artifact was not provided.
ai_behavior_issues.context: The persona says 'a paella cooking class' is booked but only the Sagrada Familia activity artifact exists in tests/agentic/artifacts/.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: When the AI gave restaurant recommendations, it proactively called create_itinerary_items to add them to the itinerary without being explicitly asked to do so. While this is arguably helpful for an organized user, it was not requested.
ai_behavior_issues.context: User asked 'Can you suggest some good restaurants near the Gothic Quarter?' and AI auto-added 3 restaurant items to the itinerary.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Booking ingestion and retrieval worked flawlessly — the AI accurately answered specific questions about terminals, check-in times, and tour details from ingested bookings. ExtractBookingField correctly returned structured data. The recommend_booking tool fired appropriately for bookable day trips and included FTC disclosure. Restaurant recommendations did not trigger recommend_booking, which is correct. The itinerary intelligently placed the Sagrada Familia tour on the correct day and built activities around existing bookings. Companion mode responses were concise and practical. Overall a strong booking-focused experience.
